name: Golden set v1.0 — 5 vendors
description: Five vendors scored by ChatGPT against rubric v1.0, with human spot-check on ~20% of dimensions. One real disclosure (Microsoft) plus four synthetic vendors designed to exercise specific failure modes.

scorecards.overall_notes: Microsoft provides strong, quantitative disclosure with clear boundaries, GHG Protocol methodology, category-level Scope 3 reporting, and external assurance for Scope 1 and 2. The main procurement caveats are that total emissions and Scope 3 emissions have increased versus the 2020 baseline, the transition plan depends partly on carbon removals and market development, and some renewable claims include unbundled EACs/RECs, which are weaker than direct PPAs or on-site generation. Disclosure quality is high, but the gap between ambition and current absolute emissions trajectory should be treated as a material risk.

scores.dimension_name: Net Zero commitment and transition plan
scores.evidence_quote: By 2030, Microsoft will cut its emissions by more than half compared to 2020 and remove more carbon than it emits.
scores.evidence_location: 2025 Environmental Sustainability Report, p.11; CDP response, section 7.54.3
scores.scoring_rationale: Microsoft discloses a dated carbon-negative target for 2030, a 2020 baseline, interim reduction targets, and annual progress against the baseline. The evidence also shows annual progress is mixed, with total Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions up 23.4% versus baseline.

scores.evidence_quote: We are reporting a Scope 2, location-based figure
scores.scoring_rationale: Microsoft discloses Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions using the GHG Protocol, with an operational-control boundary and both location-based and market-based Scope 2 figures. Score 2 not 3 because the reviewed evidence does not show a clear three-year absolute reduction trend for Scope 1 and 2.

scores.evidence_quote: Microsoft's Scope 3 emissions continue to account for more than 97% of our total emissions.
scores.evidence_location: 2025 Environmental Sustainability Report, p.13; Microsoft-2025-CDP-response.pdf, sections 7.4.1 and 7.8
scores.scoring_rationale: Microsoft discloses material Scope 3 categories, exclusions, methodologies, and category-level emissions, including supplier-specific, spend-based, and LCA methods. Score 2 not 3 because supplier engagement is evidenced but overall Scope 3 emissions increased 26% versus the 2020 baseline, so reduction impact is not yet clearly demonstrated at total Scope 3 level.

scores.evidence_quote: We engaged Deloitte & Touche LLP to perform a review in accordance with the attestation standards
scores.scoring_rationale: Microsoft reports external limited assurance by Deloitte under AICPA attestation standards for 100% of Scope 1 and both Scope 2 approaches. Score 2 not 3 because the evidence indicates limited assurance and only partial Scope 3 assurance, not reasonable assurance or AA1000AS Type 2 covering Scope 1, 2, and material Scope 3.

scores.evidence_quote: Yes, and this target has been approved by the Science Based Targets initiative
scores.scoring_rationale: Microsoft discloses SBTi-approved elements, including a Scope 3 intensity target and a renewable electricity target. Score 1 not 2 because the net-zero/carbon-negative target is explicitly marked as not science-based, and the document does not evidence an SBTi-validated near-term target covering Scope 1, Scope 2, and material Scope 3 together.

scorecards.overall_notes: Disclosure quality is generally strong, with quantified emissions data, a clear 2019 baseline, interim targets, transition levers and external limited assurance. The main conservative scoring constraints are that Scope 3 supplier engagement has not yet evidenced actual emissions reductions, and long-term Net Zero target validation by SBTi is still under review. Renewable electricity is REGO-backed, which is weaker than direct PPAs or on-site generation, but this does not materially change the Scope 1 and 2 disclosure score.

scores.dimension_name: Net Zero commitment and transition plan
scores.evidence_quote: committed to achieving Net Zero greenhouse gas emissions across our value chain by 2045
scores.evidence_location: p.3, Section 1 Net Zero Commitment and Transition Plan
scores.scoring_rationale: The report gives a Net Zero target year of 2045, a 2019 baseline, interim 2030 targets for Scope 1, 2 and material Scope 3, and states that progress is reported annually. It also outlines transition levers and committed decarbonisation capex, supporting a score of 3.

scores.evidence_quote: We disclose both location-based and market-based Scope 2 emissions
scores.evidence_location: p.4, Section 2 Scope 1 and Scope 2 Emissions
scores.scoring_rationale: The report states GHG Protocol reporting, operational control boundary, factors used, and discloses both location-based and market-based Scope 2. It provides 2019, 2022, 2023 and 2024 data showing an absolute market-based Scope 1 and 2 reduction trend, supporting a score of 3.

scores.evidence_quote: 7 are reported as material to Sentinel Records
scores.scoring_rationale: The report discloses all categories deemed material under a materiality assessment and explains why other categories are excluded. Supplier engagement is evidenced, but the report does not demonstrate that the supplier programme is already reducing Scope 3 emissions; Scope 3 is stated as behind trajectory, so score 2 rather than 3.

scores.evidence_quote: subject to limited assurance by Brackenbury Verification Services Ltd
scores.scoring_rationale: The report identifies an external assurance provider and states limited assurance under ISAE 3000 and ISAE 3410 for Scope 1, Scope 2 and selected Scope 3 categories. It does not claim reasonable assurance or coverage of all material Scope 3, so score 2.

scores.evidence_quote: near-term, validated November 2023
scores.scoring_rationale: The report explicitly states that near-term targets were validated by SBTi and shows targets covering Scope 1, 2 and material Scope 3. Long-term Net Zero validation is still under review, so this does not meet the score 3 threshold.

scorecards.overall_notes: The disclosure is transparent about its limitations, particularly the absence of an interim target, partial Scope 3 coverage, lack of external assurance and lack of SBTi validation. The document is relatively clear for a small annual report extract, but the evidence base remains immature and mostly self-reported. No significant greenwashing concern is apparent because caveats are stated plainly, although future-facing commitments should not be treated as achieved progress.

scores.dimension_name: Net Zero commitment and transition plan
scores.evidence_quote: The Board has approved a target of Net Zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050. Our baseline year is 2022
scores.scoring_rationale: The document states a Net Zero target year of 2050 and a 2022 baseline year. It does not have an interim 2030 target, so it does not meet the threshold for a score of 3.

scores.evidence_quote: Emissions are calculated in accordance with the GHG Protocol Corporate Standard.
scores.scoring_rationale: Scope 1 and location-based Scope 2 emissions are disclosed with methodology, boundary and emission factors. However, market-based Scope 2 is not disclosed, and there is not at least three years of absolute emissions trend data.

scores.evidence_quote: We have included business travel and waste generated in operations.
scores.scoring_rationale: Scope 3 disclosure is explicitly partial and limited to business travel and waste generated in operations. The company acknowledges this is only a small proportion of likely total Scope 3 emissions.

scores.evidence_quote: Our environmental data has been reviewed internally by our Finance Director and the Board's Audit Committee.
scores.scoring_rationale: The document evidences internal review only and explicitly states that no external assurance provider has been engaged. This does not meet the threshold for third-party assurance.

scores.evidence_quote: We have reviewed the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) framework and intend to seek validation
scores.scoring_rationale: The company has a stated Net Zero target, but there is no evidence of SBTi validation. Intending to seek validation is not equivalent to having SBTi-validated targets.

scorecards.overall_notes: Disclosure quality is mixed: the document provides dated Net Zero and interim reduction targets plus FY2024 Scope 1 and market-based Scope 2 figures, but it relies heavily on marketing language for Scope 3, verification, and supplier engagement. Greenwashing risk is elevated around the badges and phrases 'Carbon Neutral', 'SBTi Aligned', and 'Independently Verified', because the evidence shows offset-based neutrality, no SBTi validation yet, and internal rather than third-party assurance. Renewable electricity is based partly on unbundled RECs and Guarantees of Origin, which is a weaker claim than direct PPAs or on-site generation.

scores.dimension_name: Net Zero commitment and transition plan
scores.evidence_quote: Net Zero by 2040
scores.scoring_rationale: The document states a Net Zero target year of 2040 and a 2021 baseline, with an interim 2030 Scope 1 and 2 reduction target. It does not provide annual progress reporting against the baseline, so it does not meet the threshold for 3.

scores.evidence_quote: Scope 2 (purchased electricity, market-based)
scores.scoring_rationale: Scope 1 and market-based Scope 2 figures are disclosed and methodology is stated as aligned with the GHG Protocol. However, location-based Scope 2 is not disclosed, and there is no three-year absolute reduction trend.

scores.evidence_quote: Detailed Scope 3 category breakdown is being prepared for our forthcoming Impact Report.
scores.evidence_location: Scope 3: Engaging Our Value Chain section
scores.scoring_rationale: The document claims comprehensive Scope 3 coverage but provides no category-level breakdown, quantitative emissions data, or materiality assessment. Supplier engagement is described in general terms without evidence of emissions reduction.

scores.evidence_quote: verified annually by our internal Sustainability Audit function
scores.evidence_location: Frequently Asked Questions — Who verifies BoxVault's emissions data?
scores.scoring_rationale: Verification is performed by an internal Sustainability Audit function, not an external assurance provider under ISAE 3000, AA1000AS, or equivalent. External limited assurance is only described as a future option.

scores.evidence_quote: currently preparing a formal submission to SBTi for validation
scores.evidence_location: Frequently Asked Questions — Are BoxVault's targets validated by the Science Based Targets initiative?
scores.scoring_rationale: Targets are quantified and described as using the SBTi framework, but the document explicitly says formal SBTi validation has not yet been completed. Under the rubric, SBTi-aligned methodology does not qualify for a score of 2.

scorecards.overall_notes: Disclosure quality is weak: the document is a short web statement with broad environmental commitments but no quantitative emissions data, targets, methodology, baseline, assurance, or SBTi validation. The only scored evidence is a limited set of operational practices such as recycling, energy-efficient lighting, staff practices, and supplier improvement discussions; these do not materially support the rubric dimensions.

scores.dimension_name: Net Zero commitment and transition plan
scores.scoring_rationale: The statement expresses support for a low-carbon economy but contains no dated Net Zero commitment, baseline year, transition plan, or interim target. Carbon footprint reduction language is generic and not sufficient for a Net Zero score.
No Scope 1 or Scope 2 emissions figures are disclosed. There is also no GHG Protocol methodology, organisational boundary, or location-based/market-based Scope 2 reporting.
No Scope 3 emissions inventory, categories, or materiality assessment are disclosed. A general reference to working with suppliers is not enough to evidence Scope 3 disclosure.
The document contains no evidence of third-party assurance, assurance provider, assurance standard, or verified emissions boundary. It is a self-reported web statement only.
No quantified emissions reduction targets are disclosed, and there is no evidence of SBTi validation. The document does not claim science-based targets.
